pages.content: Watch Brands Guide: Complete Collections & Reviews – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Brands DNA GROUPS COUNTRIES CONNECTED ALPHABETICAL BRANDMARK GROUP CASIO COMPUTER CO. LTD CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD COMPAGNIE FINANCIÈRE RICHEMONT FESTINA GROUP FOSSIL GROUP INC FRANCK MULLER GROUP ICE HOLDING GROUP INVICTA WATCH GROUP LVMH MONDAINE GROUP MOVADO GROUP PARTNERS GROUP POINT TEC ROLEX SA SEIKO GROUP CORPORATION SWATCH GROUP LTD TIMEX GROUP TIMEWAY GROUP FINE JEWELLERY INDEPENDENT WATCH COMPANIES MICROBRAND AUSTRALIA AUSTRIA BELGIUM CANADA CHINA DENMARK ENGLAND FRANCE GEORGIA GERMANY GREECE HONG KONG ITALY INDIA JAPAN NETHERLANDS NORWAY PUERTO RICO SINGAPORE SPAIN SWEDEN SWITZERLAND THAILAND TAIWAN UNITED KINGDOM UNITED STATES OF AMERICA TRADITIONAL BRANDS × Breitling Breitling is a Swiss luxury watch company that was founded in 1884 by Leon Breitling. The company’s headquarters are located in Grenchen, Switzerland. Breitling is known for producing high-quality, precision watches for pilots, divers, and other professionals. The company’s watches are known for their durability, accuracy, and distinctive design, and are highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts. In addition to its headquarters in Switzerland, Breitling has boutiques and authorized dealers located in cities around the world. Read More × Girard Perregaux The Girard-Perregaux watch brand was established in 1791, making it one of the oldest luxury watch manufacturers in Switzerland. The brand is well-known for its sophisticated watches as well as the technological improvements that it has made during its long history of invention and excellence in the watchmaking industry. During the course of its history, the brand is responsible for a number of significant advancements in horology, including the invention of the wristwatch and numerous other significant timekeeping advances. The timepieces produced by Girard-Perregaux are renowned for their unmatched elegance, outstanding craftsmanship, and meticulous attention to detail. Collectors and watch aficionados all around the globe have an insatiable appetite for the brand’s collections, which include everything from traditional dress watches to timepieces designed for sports and diving. Read More × Hublot Hublot is a luxury watch brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1980 by Carlo Crocco. The brand is known for its bold, contemporary designs, and its use of advanced materials, such as carbon fiber and ceramic. Hublot is particularly well-known for its “Big Bang” collection, which features watches with a distinctive, layered case design, as well as its “Classic Fusion” collection, which is inspired by traditional watchmaking styles. Hublot is also known for its collaborations with other luxury brands and personalities, such as Ferrari, and Pelé, and for its commitment to traditional watchmaking techniques. Hublot watches are highly sought-after by collectors and enthusiasts for their unique style, and craftsmanship. Read More × Ice Watch Ice-Watch is a bold and accessible watch brand that emerged in the entry-level fashion timepiece segment. Founded by Jean-Pierre Lutgen in late 2006 and distributed globally since 2007, it fills the niche for colourful, stylish watches that feel both youthful and universally appealing. Known for its vibrant blend of retro-modern

pages.title: About WatchDNA | Educate, Entertain & Connect Watch Enthusiasts
pages.content: About WatchDNA | Educate, Entertain & Connect Watch Enthusiasts Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Your browser does not support the video tag. We aim to build a vibrant community where people can discover new brands and meet jewellers and aficionados passionate about timepieces and their stories. Here at WatchDNA, we recognize how essential it is to have all the knowledge required before purchasing. While our goal is to promote the item sold by our jewellery and brand partners, we strongly encourage everyone to learn as much as possible about each brand. Our brand portfolio constantly evolves, and we always search for new names to add. All items on our website are offered either directly by one of our jewelry partners or by the brand itself. You can follow the provided link to order directly from their website, or if you prefer, feel free to contact your nearest store to try them out in person. For added convenience, we also offer the service of transferring pre-selected items to the WatchDNA jeweller closest to you. While we strive to collaborate with local businesses, we also maintain strong relationships with certain manufacturers. Please be advised that for specific brands, we will redirect you directly to their own e-commerce platforms, and as a result, those brands cannot be physically tried in person. In addition, we take pride in making a positive impact on society, which is why we have partnered with exceptional nonprofits, and continually looking for additional local organizations to support. Get involved, discover the ideal timepiece or accessory, and help shape the world for future generations. TIME IS NOW! CREATE A WORLD FULL OF GIVING WITH US. "BE THE CHANGE YOU WANT TO SEE IN THE WORLD." GANDHI BE PART OF THE SOLUTION We all have our paths on this planet, and it’s our belief that discovering and pursuing our unique missions is vital to experiencing fulfillment. That’s why we’ve chosen to become part of B1G1 and 1% FOR THE PLANET ‘s global communities. By doing so, we aim to expand and enhance our community involvement as we journey through life. Living by our motto: Life is too short; one must enjoy it, Explore its kingdom in its immensity. Don't stop at what you believe to see, But on the contrary, search without any despair. Support our community Using Gandhi's quote as inspiration, we are firmly convinced that each of us can make a difference in our communities Fight climate change Our next generation deserves a better future, so we must work together to contribute to a Clean Earth. Check out below the non-for-profit organization that we decided to support. WatchDNA has committed to partnering with amazing non-profit organizations to support its mission of creating a world full of giving. Together, we could make a significant difference in the world and be part of the solution. For every transaction you make, something amazing happens in the world. TIME IS NOW! pages.content: Watchmaking – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping WATCHMAKING 101 OUR GOAL IS TO CREATE A PLATFORM WHERE EVERYONE WHO WANTS TO LEARN ABOUT TIMEPIECES FEELS WELCOME We are here to help you on your quest to discover all things watches. With the expertise of watchmakers in our community we have produced an easy-to-understand glossary of watch terms to assist you in acquiring a better understanding of timepiece mechanics. Understanding the DNA of a watch can help you make informed decisions when it comes to choosing the right timepiece for you. Bezel The bezel is the ring surrounding the dial and crystal. Good to know: Some bezels rotate and are used for timing activities such as diving or measuring speed with a tachymeter scale . Crystal The crystal is the transparent cover that protects the dial while allowing you to read the time. Good to know: Most modern watches use sapphire crystal , which is highly scratch-resistant and extremely durable. Movement The movement is the internal mechanism that powers the watch and keeps time. Good to know: Movements can be mechanical (automatic or manual) or quartz , each offering different benefits in precision and craftsmanship. Bracelet or Strap The bracelet or strap is the band that holds the watch on your wrist. Good to know: Watches may come with metal bracelets, leather straps, rubber straps, or fabric , each suited for different styles and activities. Bezel The bezel is the ring surrounding the dial and crystal. Good to know: Some bezels rotate and are used for timing activities such as diving or measuring speed with a tachymeter scale . Crystal The crystal is the transparent cover that protects the dial while allowing you to read the time. Good to know: Most modern watches use sapphire crystal , which is highly scratch-resistant and extremely durable. Movement The movement is the internal mechanism that powers the watch and keeps time. Good to know: Movements can be mechanical (automatic or manual) or quartz , each offering different benefits in precision and craftsmanship. Bracelet or Strap The bracelet or strap is the band that holds the watch on your wrist. Good to know: Watches may come with metal bracelets, leather straps, rubber straps, or fabric , each suited for different styles and activities. Dial The dial is the face of the watch where the time is displayed with markers and details. Good to know: Dials can feature luminescent materials so you can read the time in low light. Hands The hands are the moving pointers that indicate the hours, minutes, and sometimes seconds. Good to know: Many watches use luminous coating on the hands so they glow in the dark. Crown The crown is the small knob on the side of the watch used to set the time and date. Good to know: On many sport watches, the crown is screw-down to help improve water resistance. Case The case is the main body of the watch that protects all the internal components. Good to know: Most quality watches use stainless steel, titanium, ceramic, or precious metals for durability and comfort. One Day. One Global Community.” On October 10, the world will celebrate World Watch Day, a day dedicated to the art, culture, and passion of watchmaking. From seasoned collectors to first-time enthusiasts, from master artisans to curious newcomers, this is a global invitation to pause, connect, and share in the timeless beauty of horology. Through local gatherings, online events, inspiring stories, and wrist shots shared across the globe, World Watch Day will highlight how watches are far more than instruments of timekeeping; they are symbols of heritage, innovation, and human creativity. Join the movement, discover the stories behind the timepieces, and be part of a worldwide celebration where every second counts. #WorldWatchDay ARTICLES ABOUT WORLD WATCH DAY WORLD WATCH DAY — WHY CELEBRATE TIMEPIECES? pages.content: RedBar – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping RedBar Redbar, a globally recognized watch enthusiast group, began as a modest gathering in New York City and has evolved into an international community of horology lovers. Founded by Adam Craniotes , @Craniotes , and a few dedicated watch enthusiasts, Redbar's mission was to create a judgment-free, pretentious-free space for people to bond over their shared love for watches, enjoy drinks, and form lasting friendships. Initially, small, informal meetings were held at a bar that gave the group its name. Over time, the gatherings grew, and with the help of social media, Redbar's popularity soared, attracting members from various walks of life. Today, Redbar NYC meets monthly, inspiring the formation of chapters in cities like Toronto, London, Bombay, Edinburgh, and Dallas/Fort Worth. With over 85 chapters worldwide, Redbar continues to maintain its core values of inclusivity, camaraderie, and a shared passion for horology, offering a welcoming space for both seasoned collectors and new enthusiasts to explore the world of watches. pages.content: Media Directory – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Editorial Highlights and Curated Content Below, you’ll discover curated synopses of articles from respected media platforms. While the full articles are published on our partners’ websites, we proudly feature a brief overview here to help you explore their insights and perspectives. Each synopsis includes a direct link to the original publication. These collaborations are built on trust and shared values, as we continue working alongside media partners who help educate, inform, and inspire the global watch community. ‹ SOVRYGN WATCHES: A JOURNEY OF PASSION AND PERSEVERANCE February 24, 2026 GEORGE SULLY: FROM CANADIAN DESIGN ICON TO ADVOCATE AND STORYTELLER January 25, 2026 › Dedicated Watch Media Dedicated watch media are specialized publications focused exclusively on horology. They provide in-depth technical analysis, historical context, hands-on reviews, and industry insights tailored to collectors and enthusiasts. These outlets often shape collector opinion, influence brand perception, and serve as authoritative voices within the global watch community. A Timely Perspective A blog to watch Escapement Magazine Fratello Watches Hodinkee Monochrome Neue Uhren🇩🇪 Orafix🇮🇹 Passion horlogère🇫🇷 Quill & Pad Revolution Swiss Watches Magazine The Calibrated Wrist Time + Tide Time Square Magazine🇹🇼 Watch Collecting Lifestyle Watch Journal Watchonista Watch Pro Watch Time Watch Your Time Worn & Wound Wrist Enthusiast Lifestyle & fashion media Lifestyle & fashion media approach watches through the lens of design, style, and elevated living. Coverage emphasizes aesthetics, craftsmanship, brand storytelling, celebrity associations, and cultural relevance, positioning timepieces as symbols of achievement, refined taste, and personal identity. Rather than focusing purely on technical specifications, these outlets frame watches within the broader world of wealth, travel, automobiles, and fine living. Their readership—often composed of high-net-worth individuals and aspirational consumers—makes them powerful platforms for brand elevation, desirability, and lifestyle-driven visibility. BDC Magazine Collection Magazine Elle Elle Canada Elle Quebec🇫🇷 Fairmont Magazine Fashion Magazine Gear Patrol Globe & Mail Style magazine Glory Sports GQ Haute Living Hollywood Reporter Nuvo Magazine Robb Report Sharp Magazine The Kit Financial & Business Media Financial and business media analyze watches as economic assets and strategic industry players. Coverage may include market performance, brand acquisitions, resale value trends, and investment potential. These platforms position watches within the global luxury economy and appeal to executives, investors, and business-minded readers. Forbes Trade & Industry Media (B2B) Trade and industry publications serve professionals within the watch and jewelry ecosystem, including brands, authorized dealers, distributors, and suppliers. Their content focuses on retail strategy, distribution networks, product launches, trade shows, and regulatory developments. These outlets are essential for B2B visibility and industry credibility. pages.title:
brands by Group
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: brands by Group – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping DISCOVER ALL WATCH GROUPS At WatchDNA, our mission is to Educate , Entertain , and Connect the global watch community. One of the first insights we uncovered is that many people don’t realize most watch brands belong to larger watch groups—each uniting a range of brands, resources, and horological expertise under one roof. This page is designed to help you explore these groups and understand their influence on the industry. We hope you learn something new—and enjoy the journey through the world of watches. CASIO COMPUTER CO. LTD Casio, founded in 1946, became a digital watch pioneer with the 1974 CASIOTRON and cemented its legacy through iconic innovations like G-Shock, Pro Trek, Edifice, and the luxury-focused Oceanus. CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD Citizen Watch Co. Ltd., founded in 1918, has grown into a global horological powerhouse known for Eco-Drive technology and innovation, with a diverse portfolio that includes Citizen, Bulova, Accutron, Frederique Constant, Alpina, Campanola, and Arnold & Son. COMPAGNIE FINANCIÈRE RICHEMONT SA Compagnie Financière Richemont SA, founded in 1988 by Johann Rupert, is a global luxury powerhouse that owns a diverse portfolio of historic watch and jewelry Maisons, including Cartier , Vacheron Constantin , and IWC , and is renowned for preserving craftsmanship while embracing innovation. FESTINA GROUP The Festina Group , a Spanish watch conglomerate founded in 1984, owns and operates several global watch brands—including Festina , Jaguar , Candino , Lotus , and Calypso —offering a range of Swiss-made and fashion-forward timepieces across various price points, with a strong heritage in sports marketing and cycling. FOSSIL GROUP INC The Fossil Group, founded in 1984, has grown into one of the world’s largest watch manufacturers, producing both in-house and licensed timepieces for brands like Armani Exchange , Diesel , DKNY , Kate Spade , Michele , Zodiac , and Skagen , each representing a unique design identity and market segment. FRANCK MULLER GROUP The Franck Muller Group, though small in size, is a respected Swiss watchmaking powerhouse that includes Franck Muller, Pierre Kunz, and Backes & Strauss, each known for bold designs, technical mastery, and distinctive identities. INVICTA WATCH GROUP Invicta Watch Group traces its origins to 1837, when Raphael Picard founded the company in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland. Originally dedicated to crafting high-quality mechanical watches at accessible prices, the brand’s name— Invicta , meaning “invincible” in Latin—has long represented resilience and innovation in watchmaking. LVMH | Louis Vuitton Moët Hennessy LVMH, while best known for fashion, champagne, and spirits, has become a major force in watchmaking through its ownership of innovative and iconic brands like TAG Heuer , Tiffany & Co , Hublot , Zenith , Bulgari , Chaumet , and Daniel Roth . MONDAINE GROUP The Mondaine Group is a family-owned Swiss watchmaker that oversees four distinctive brands—Mondaine, Luminox, Pierre Cardin, and M-Watch—each tailored to different audiences, from iconic rail-inspired designs to rugged tactical gear and affordable fashion watches. MOVADO GROUP INC. The Movado Group Inc. is a leading global watch company that strategically combines heritage and fashion through its owned br

pages.title:
Platforms
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Platforms – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping EXPLORE WATCH EDUCATION PLATFORMS Across the global watchmaking landscape, numerous independent platforms are dedicated to educating, documenting, and preserving horological knowledge. These resources offer valuable insights into watch history, craftsmanship, technical skills, and industry standards, helping both enthusiasts and professionals expand their understanding of the craft. This page highlights key educational platforms that contribute to the growth of the watch community by fostering learning, promoting transparency, and making expert information accessible worldwide. Switzerland HOROPEDIA Horopedia is a free, independent online video encyclopaedia dedicated to the art, science and culture of watchmaking, governed by the Horopedia Foundation , a Swiss public-interest non-profit organisation based in Geneva. The platform aims to preserve, document and share horological knowledge — from technical skills and historical context to core principles and professional practice — with students, professionals and enthusiasts worldwide. SWISS PRODIG Swiss Prodig is a professional Swiss industrial directory and B2B networking platform designed to connect manufacturers, subcontractors and service providers across high-precision sectors such as horology, micro-mechanics, MedTech, aerospace, electronics and industrial engineering. The platform allows companies to showcase their profiles, compare capabilities and engage directly with qualified partners for collaborative projects. HOROPEDIA The mission of the Horopedia Foundation includes educating and supporting understanding of watchmaking through rich multimedia content; producing and curating over 100 multilingual documentary films on museums, watchmaking schools, tools, components and mechanical principles; and maintaining nearly 1,000 detailed glossary pages explaining technical terms, mechanisms and historical developments. Content is provided in French, English and German. Learn more SWISS PRODIG The platform is operated by Swiss Prodig Sàrl, a limited liability company headquartered in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland, with a business focus on creating, managing and developing a digital ecosystem for industrial supply-chain visibility and collaboration. Swiss Prodig’s mission is to make the Swiss industrial and subcontracting landscape more transparent, efficient and accessible by curating a reliable, manually verified directory of companies and by offering advanced search filters, comparison tools and direct contact functionalities that streamline partner identification for buyers and project managers. Learn more 1 OF 2 While not all groups are currently featured on our site, our directory is continually evolving as we expand and update our listings. Please feel free to reach out if you would like your organization to be listed, or if you are a member of an organization that supports industry stakeholders. pages.title:
Committee
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Committee – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping DISCOVER WATCH INDUSTRY COMMITTEES Within the global watchmaking ecosystem, several committees, associations, and organizations play a vital role in supporting local brands and industry stakeholders. These institutions help companies connect within their own countries and internationally, participate in global trade shows, strengthen industry visibility, and promote national craftsmanship on the world stage. This page introduces some of the key committees and associations dedicated to empowering watch brands and fostering cross-border collaboration. Canada CANADIAN JEWELLERS ASSOCIATION Canadian Jewellers Association (CJA) is the national trade association representing the Canadian jewellery and watch industry. Its origins date back to the early 20th century, when professionals in the field sought a unified voice to strengthen and protect the trade in Canada. CANADIAN JEWELLERY GROUP Canadian Jewellery Group (CJG) is Canada’s largest jewellery buying cooperative, empowering independent jewellers through collective purchasing and shared resources since its establishment in 1977. The organization was formed by seven jewellers from northern Ontario who collaborated to improve their purchasing power and share the benefits of negotiated supplier terms, rebates, and discounts with members across the country. CANADIAN JEWELLERS ASSOCIATION The organization traces its roots to the Jewellers Security Alliance of Canada, founded in 1884 to help improve security practices and combat theft. This initiative later evolved into the Canadian National Jewellers Association, which held its first official meeting on September 25, 1918 . By 1921, the Association had introduced its first Code of Ethics, and in 1922 it formally adopted the name Canadian Jewellers Association (CJA) . Since then, the CJA has remained committed to education, advocacy, and promoting ethical business standards across the jewellery and watch sectors. The CJA is recognized as a key authority within the Canadian jewellery and watch trade, guiding members through its Code of Ethics, educational programs, and advocacy efforts. Its work supports industry growth, strengthens consumer confidence, and ensures best practices across all areas of the jewellery sector. Learn more CANADIAN JEWELLERY GROUP Over the decades, the CJG has grown into a nationally recognized group with retail members operating numerous jewellery stores throughout Canada. By harnessing the collective buying strength of its members, the Group provides access to premium products from North America’s top suppliers while allowing independent retailers to maintain their unique identities and local presence. The organization negotiates favorable terms and conditions with endorsed suppliers and passes 100% of those advantages on to its members, including volume rebates, exclusive payment options, and centralized billing handled by the CJG head office. It also hosts an annual buying show and facilitates networking, education, and collaboration opportunities for its membership, reinforcing the competitive position of independent jewellers in the national market. Learn more 1 OF 2 France FRANCECLAT Francéclat is the professional committee dedicated to supporting and promoting France’s watchmaking, jewelry, and tableware industries—

pages.title: WatchDNA | Your Daily Routine
pages.content: WatchDNA | Your Daily Routine Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Your Daily Routine What started as a photography project—rooted in our founder’s first passion for capturing the daily lives of strangers he crossed paths with—has now evolved into something even more meaningful. You may have seen some of his early work on his personal account, @LiveandCapture . We’re now inviting you to be part of this growing story: Share a photo of your wrist and the timepiece you’re wearing, along with your location and the date. This simple gesture allows us—and our community—to “pause” time for just a moment. It’s a powerful reminder of how quickly life moves and how meaningful it is when we take time to reflect, connect, and create something together. Let’s come together and showcase the beauty of shared moments and collective action—one wristshot at a time. ✨ And if you’d like, feel free to add a personal comment with your submission—we’d love to hear what time means to you. ⏱️ Want to go even deeper? If you wish to participate more fully in this project, we welcome you to reach out and submit a short article about how timepieces and the act of keeping time play a role in your daily routine and everyday life. Your story might be featured in an upcoming post or article. Discover our community post #WatchDNADailyRoutine CAPTURES FROM OUR COMMUNITY Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Opens in a new window.

pages.content: B1g1 business for good – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping B1g1 business for good THE STORY OF B1G1 In 2007, B1G1 started with a simple idea: “ What if every business could make a difference in their own way, just by doing what they normally do? ”. With more than a decade of work, this simple idea has now become a global movement. TOTAL GIVING IMPACTS WHAT ARE THE SDGS? On 25 September 2015, world leaders from all sectors convened to adopt the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). They represent the global community’s next step in ending poverty, fighting inequality and injustice, and tackling climate change for the next 15 years. Here at B1G1, we feel motivated by these collective indicators too. There is now a greater call for businesses to take much-needed actions. LEARN MORE TIME IS NOW! Watches & Wonders Show
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Watches & Wonders Show – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Watches & Wonders Show Watches and Wonders began its journey in Geneva in 1991, originally known as the Salon International de la Haute Horlogerie (SIHH). Over the years, it has grown into one of the most prestigious events in the watchmaking world, bringing together the finest maisons and industry professionals. In 2020, it was rebranded as Watches and Wonders, expanding its global footprint with the addition of Watches and Wonders Shanghai. Today, both Geneva and Shanghai serve as key stages for celebrating the art, innovation, and heritage of fine watchmaking on an international scale. Discover Watchesandwonders.com ARTICLES ABOUT THE WATCH & WONDERS SHOW ORKOS: REVOLUTIONIZING LUXURY WATCH SECURITY WITH PATENTED CLASP TECHNOLOGY U-BOAT CELEBRATES 25 YEARS OF BOLD HOROLOGY AT WATCHES & WONDERS 2025 WATCHES & WONDERS 2025: THE ULTIMATE FAN'S GUIDE TO HOROLOGICAL FIREWORKS VACHERON CONSTANTIN REDEFINES HOROLOGICAL EXCELLENCE WITH THE LES CABINOTIERS SOLARIA ULTRA GRAND COMPLICATION WATCHES & WONDERS GENEVA DAY 1: 10 HOT NEW RELEASES YOU NEED TO SEE! pages.content: Windup Watch Fair – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Windup Watch Fair The Windup Watch Fair is one of the most dynamic and accessible watch events in the world, bringing together independent brands, seasoned collectors, and curious newcomers under one roof. Organized by Worn & Wound , this free-to-attend fair offers a unique hands-on experience where attendees can explore timepieces up close, connect directly with brand founders, and immerse themselves in the vibrant culture of modern watchmaking. Whether you’re a long-time enthusiast or just starting your horological journey, Windup is the perfect place to discover new brands, hear untold stories, and join a passionate global community. Under the patronage of Sheikha Latifa bint Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, the show showcases more than 90 global watch brands. The event brings together collectors, industry leaders, and watch enthusiasts for an inspiring program of masterclasses, expert forums, brand exhibitions, and immersive experiences. More than just a trade fair, Dubai Watch Week is a cultural platform that fosters dialogue, creativity, and appreciation for the art and innovation of fine watchmaking. pages.content: The Canadian Watches & Jewelry Show – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The Canadian Watches & Jewelry Show The Canadian Watches & Jewelry Show is a one-day industry and enthusiast event held in Laval, near Montreal, bringing together leading exhibitors of luxury timepieces and fine jewelry. Designed for both seasoned collectors and curious newcomers, the show offers a curated showcase of watch brands and jewelry designers, highlighting craftsmanship, design, and innovation. It serves as a key moment for the Canadian watch and jewelry community to connect, discover new collections, and celebrate the artistry behind horology and fine accessories. The Couture Show
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: The Couture Show – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The Couture Show COUTURE is a premier trade event held annually in Las Vegas, bringing together a curated selection of the world’s finest jewelry designers, luxury timepiece brands, and innovative artists. Known for its intimate yet upscale environment, COUTURE is designed for industry professionals who value exclusivity, artistry, and craftsmanship. Attendees include a diverse mix of independent retailers, global designers, and brands who gather to network, forge business partnerships, and explore new trends in design. The event offers a blend of networking opportunities, educational sessions, and immersive exhibits, creating a space for collaboration, inspiration, and celebration of the best in fine jewelry and luxury watches.  – WatchDNA
pages.content: EPHJ – The International Trade Show for High Precision – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping EPHJ – The International Trade Show for High Precision EPHJ – Le monde de la haute précision is the world’s leading international trade show dedicated to high-precision industries. Held annually in Geneva, EPHJ brings together the key players of watchmaking, jewellery, microtechnology and medtech , creating a unique ecosystem where tradition meets innovation. The show is designed as a true business and networking platform, connecting brands, suppliers, manufacturers, engineers and decision-makers from across the globe. EPHJ showcases cutting-edge technologies, components, materials and processes that define the future of high-precision craftsmanship and industrial excellence. More than an exhibition, EPHJ is a place for exchange, discovery and collaboration, where visitors can explore new solutions, identify emerging trends and build long-term partnerships within a highly specialized and demanding industry. Discover more about the show: Visit the official EPHJ page ARTICLES ABOUT EPHJ – LE MONDE DE LA HAUTE PRÉCISION EPHJ: THE GLOBAL EPICENTER OF HIGH PRECISION AND INDUSTRIAL INNOVATION Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. pages.content: Time to Watches – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Time to Watches Since its launch in 2022, Time to Watches has established itself as one of the most dynamic international watch events, uniting luxury watch brands, Swiss manufacturers, independent watchmakers, and microbrands with their global audiences. More than just a trade show, it is a platform for product launches, brand storytelling, professional networking, and authentic engagement with collectors, retailers, media, influencers, and watch enthusiasts. The flagship Swiss edition is held every April during Geneva Watch Week, while the U.S. edition in Las Vegas each May focuses on the North American market. From haute horlogerie to avant-garde creations, Time to Watches fosters an atmosphere that blends innovation, craftsmanship, and authenticity. With its informal yet professional style, the event highlights product excellence, creativity, and meaningful connections that extend beyond traditional boundaries. Whether you are a watch brand seeking visibility, a collector discovering the next hidden gem, or a retailer exploring opportunities, Time to Watches is where the global watch industry connects, inspires, and evolves. Discover more at the official website: timetowatches.com ARTICLES ABOUT THE TIME TO WATCHES EVENT A NEW ERA FOR HAUTE HORLOGERIE IN THE U.S.: COUTURE & TIME TO WATCHES JOIN FORCES Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. The awards recognize timepiece design achievement in 11 different categories and an additional people's choice category for the finalists of all the categories. The finalist grouping of each category and winning selection of each finalist group are determined by a confidential vote of the selected Timepiece World award 2025 committee members and jurors. The 2025 list of award-winners will be announced at the 1st annual Timepiece World awards ceremony that will take place on Saturday, September 20, 2025 in Toronto, Canada. pages.content: The Temporis International Awards – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The Temporis International Awards The Temporis International Awards 2025, founded by Dan Vardie, are a global celebration of innovation, craftsmanship, and storytelling in contemporary watchmaking. Now in its 12th edition, the awards honor excellence across 12 diverse categories, including sustainable, connected, complication, and everyday watches. What sets TIA apart is its unique hybrid model: a panel of international experts selects the finalists, while the global public casts votes online to determine the winners. The 2025 edition will take place in Zurich on October 2, with voting already open for the “Masterpiece” category, which highlights standout pieces from Watches & Wonders Geneva. Discover Temporis.watch ARTICLES ABOUT TEMPORIS INTERNATIONAL AWARDS Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Opens in a new window.

pages.title:
Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG)
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) Founded in 2001, the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) is often called “the Oscars of watchmaking,” celebrating the year’s most exceptional creations and promoting watchmaking art worldwide. Held every November in Geneva, it features around 20 awards across categories such as Ladies’, Men’s, Chronograph, Tourbillon, Sports, Jewellery, Artistic Crafts, and Iconic, plus special prizes like Petite Aiguille, Eco-Innovation, and Horological Revelation. Finalists are shortlisted by the GPHG Academy and judged by a 30-member international jury of experts. The 2024 edition, hosted at Geneva’s Théâtre du Léman, saw IWC’s Portugieser Eternal Calendar win the Aiguille d’Or, Van Cleef & Arpels sweep the women’s categories, and independents like Rémy Cools and Ming take top honors. Chopard also earned the inaugural Eco-Innovation prize, reflecting the GPHG’s growing focus on sustainability. Blending tradition and innovation, GPHG remains the premier annual celebration of fine watchmaking. Discover gphg.org ARTICLES ABOUT GRAND PRIX D’HORLOGERIE DE GENÈVE A WATCH THAT HAS TRUE SWISS DNA IN EVERY ELEMENT TOP TRENDS OF 2025 GPHG 2025: DISCOVER THE 90 COMPETING TIMEPIECES AND THE WORLD TOUR STAGES CAROL BESLER GPHG PICKS, AND A LOOK AT THE NEW ECO CATEGORY FOR THE 2025 ACADEMY AWARDS OF WATCHMAKING Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. pages.title:
The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition, organized by the HKTDC alongside the Hong Kong Watch Manufacturers Association and the Federation of Hong Kong Watch Trades & Industries, runs concurrently with the 2025 Hong Kong Watch & Clock Fair and invites talent to shine in two categories—an Open Group (for individual designers or Hong Kong-registered companies) and a Student Group—each inspired by distinct themes (“Memorable” for Open and “Believe In Yourself” for Student). The competition offers winners up to HK$15,000, trophies, certificates, watch courses, media exposure, and opportunities to launch their designs to market. With applications closing on 30 April 2025, the evaluation process includes initial judging in mid-May, mock-up production through June–July, final judging in mid-July, culminating in an award presentation in early September—making it a dynamic platform to celebrate creativity, innovation, and emerging horological talent in Hong Kong. Discover hktdc.com ARTICLES ABOUT THE 42ND HONG KONG WATCH & CLOCK DESIGN COMPETITION Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Opens in a new window.

pages.title:
FAQ'S
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: FAQ'S – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping FAQ'S FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS What is WatchDNA.com, and what does it offer? WatchDNA.com is a global platform that connects the watch community by offering a comprehensive directory of watch brands, authorized dealers, and accessories. It also features educational content, watch reviews, store spotlights, and interviews with industry leaders. Our goal is to educate, entertain, and connect watch enthusiasts, collectors, and brands worldwide. Whether you’re looking for a new timepiece, discovering emerging brands, or learning about the latest trends, WatchDNA.com is your trusted resource for all things watches. Why can I not buy directly on WatchDNA site? WatchDNA is designed as a platform to bridge the gap between watch enthusiasts, brands, and their network of Authorized Dealers. We share a deep passion for timepieces and the unique stories behind their owners. We believe strongly in fostering trust and supporting local businesses, which play an essential role in their communities. That’s why WatchDNA is built as a commission-free platform , enabling you—the watch enthusiast—to connect directly with Authorized Dealers, engage with them, and purchase with confidence. This approach not only strengthens local retailers but also ensures that you benefit from expert guidance and authentic service, while discovering your next timepiece from those who best understand the craftsmanship and value behind every watch. Does WatchDNA offer watch certification? While we are not responsible for the authenticity of these timepieces, we only showcase products available through our trusted network of business partners. In addition, we are proud to collaborate with Watch Certificate™ , which, through their network of certified watchmakers, provides an additional layer of trust and assurance for your timepieces. To learn more about Watch Certificate™, you can connect directly with Trillium Watch Service. Are all watch brands featured on WatchDNA? At WatchDNA, we value informed decisions when purchasing timepieces. While not all brands are available within our jeweller network, we’re happy to connect you directly with brands that sell exclusively online. If you’re searching for a brand or model not featured on our site, simply reach out , and we’ll do our best to connect you with a trusted jeweller partner or the brand itself. Our selection is constantly growing, and we look forward to being part of your journey to the perfect timepiece. Your quest for the perfect timepiece is a journey we're excited to be a part of. I already buy watches from my local store. What could WatchDNA bring me? We're delighted to hear you've established a trusted relationship with your local jeweler— supporting local businesses is at the heart of what we do at WatchDNA. If your favorite store isn't yet featured on our platform, we'd be honored if you could introduce us. Our aim is to ensure they can extend their high-quality service to more local shoppers like you through our site. Additionally, WatchDNA is continually updated with the latest news from brands and captivating stories within our industry. We invite you to visit our site regularly to stay informed and connected with the world of timmepieces. When a new jeweller joins our platform, we make a contribution to a worthy cause,

pages.title:
STUDIO UNDERD0G BRINGS ITS “AVOCADO” ENERGY TO VANCOUVER
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: STUDIO UNDERD0G BRINGS ITS “AVOCADO” ENERGY TO VANCOUVER – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The creative and playful spirit of Studio Underd0g made itself known again with the presentation of one of its most recent and colorful proposals: the Avocado and Guacamole models. During a brief conversation with Ida, a representative of the brand, the independent studio spoke about these new pieces, which perfectly reflect the philosophy of the company, where fun design coexists with contemporary watchmaking. The inspiration behind the model is closely linked to the natural surroundings of Vancouver. The city is known for its proximity to nature; many of its residents spend their free time hiking or exploring the parks and mountains that surround it. That connection with the outdoors is reflected in the striking green color of the watch, a tone that, according to Ida, has been very well received by enthusiasts. The design immediately stands out for its aesthetics. The different shades of green visually recall avocado and guacamole, translating a culinary reference into a surprisingly elegant and original dial. As happens with many Studio Underd0g watches, the result is a balanced mix of creativity, humor, and attention to detail. During the conversation, Ida also commented that this was the first time the brand had visited Vancouver, although they had previously been in Canada, specifically in Toronto. For the team, the experience has been very positive, not only because of the city itself but also because of the community of watch enthusiasts they have been able to meet. The philosophy of the brand Studio Underd0g was founded by Richard Benc with the idea of creating a brand that would stand out for its creativity within the watch industry. The name reflects precisely that spirit, an independent brand that, like an “underdog,” competes with fresh and original proposals. Even the use of the number 0 instead of the letter “o” seeks to differentiate the brand and reinforce its unique identity. Overall, proposals such as Avocado and Guacamole clearly show the creative direction of Studio Underd0g . With bold designs, unexpected inspiration, and a strong visual personality, the brand continues to establish itself as one of the most original voices in contemporary watchmaking. pages.title:
FROM RENNES TO TAG HEUER: A SMALL WORLD IN WATCHMAKING
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: FROM RENNES TO TAG HEUER: A SMALL WORLD IN WATCHMAKING – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The watch industry is full of fascinating stories. Some are about incredible timepieces. Others are about the people behind them. And sometimes, those stories connect in the most unexpected ways. This week, it was announced that Béatrice Goasglas has been appointed the new CEO of TAG Heuer after serving as President of the Americas. For those who have followed her career, this appointment feels like a natural next step. Over the years, she has built an impressive trajectory across marketing, digital transformation, and customer experience before leading the Americas region. But what made this news feel especially personal to me is a small coincidence that reminds me how incredibly small the world can be. I was born in Paris, but when I was 14 years old, my family moved to Rennes , in Brittany. Rennes is a beautiful city, historic, vibrant, and home to Rennes School of Business , one of the most international business schools in France. Years later, after traveling the world, life brought me back to Rennes in the most unexpected way: it’s where I met my wife , who at the time was an exchange student at Rennes School of Business . And here comes the twist. While reading about Béatrice Goasglas’ career, I discovered that she also studied at Rennes School of Business around 2004–2005 . Suddenly, a simple industry headline felt strangely personal. Two students. Same city. Same school. Same time. One goes on to build a global career and eventually becomes CEO of one of Switzerland’s most iconic watch brands . The other meets her future husband there, brings him to Canada, and years later he launches WatchDNA — a platform dedicated to connecting the global watch community. Different paths. Same city. Same school. Same passion for an industry that connects people across borders. Moments like this remind me that the watch industry — despite being global — is still a remarkably human and interconnected world . So congratulations to Béatrice Goasglas on this incredible milestone. From Rennes to the global stage of Swiss watchmaking — it’s a journey that perfectly reflects what makes this industry so special. The world of watches is big… but the community behind it is wonderfully small. pages.title:
AN EVENING WITH HAMILTON: A BRAND CLOSE TO MY HEART
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: AN EVENING WITH HAMILTON: A BRAND CLOSE TO MY HEART – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping By Thomas J. Sandrin, MBA One of the greatest joys of working in the watch industry is discovering brands, their heritage, their people, and the stories behind the timepieces. "The Murph" featured in the movie INTERSTELLAR Over the years, I’ve had the privilege of meeting many extraordinary brands, but one in particular will always hold a special place in my heart: Hamilton . Hamilton is the brand that truly opened the door for me to explore the universe of watchmaking. It gave me my first real opportunity to immerse myself in the industry and understand what makes horology so fascinating. I also had the immense privilege of working closely with the brand for 12 years, collaborating with incredible teams across Canada, the United States, and Switzerland. So when the Hamilton team invited me to attend their 2026 annual review, I knew I had to be there—especially after hearing that two of my former colleagues from headquarters would also be present. What followed was an evening filled with nostalgia, discovery, and genuine appreciation for a brand that continues to evolve while staying true to its DNA. Celebrating Hamilton’s Heritage Hamilton’s heritage continues to shape its modern identity. Founded in 1892 in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, Hamilton quickly built a reputation for precision and reliability. The brand originally gained recognition producing highly accurate watches for American railroads, earning the nickname “The Watch of Railroad Accuracy.” Throughout its history, Hamilton has played important roles in aviation, military timekeeping, and eventually Swiss watchmaking after joining the Swatch Group family. Today, the brand perfectly blends American spirit with Swiss craftsmanship. But beyond its heritage, Hamilton has always stood out for its ability to connect with culture, storytelling, and innovation. “It was such a pleasure to see my old team again and to discover some of the latest collections in a setting that truly reflected Hamilton’s DNA.” A Team That Truly Represents the Brand The Canadian team once again did an exceptional job representing the brand. From the atmosphere of the evening to the presentation of the collections, everything reflected the true spirit of Hamilton —accessible, authentic, and rooted in storytelling. It was also wonderful to reconnect not only with the team but with members of the local watch community. Events like these are a reminder that watches are about far more than mechanics—they’re about people, passion, and shared enthusiasm. Discovering the Latest Collections Among the evening’s highlights: standout Pan Europ references and recent releases. During the evening, we had the opportunity to explore several exciting pieces from Hamilton ’s latest releases. Among the highlights were new interpretations of the American Classic collection as well as limited editions within the Pan Europ line. The Pan Europ Day Date Auto and Auto Chrono models were particularly interesting, especially given their appearance in the latest video game Resident Evil Requiem, demonstrating Hamilton ’s growing presence in the gaming world. This evolution feels like a natural extension of the brand’s long-standing relationship with entertainment. Hamilton and the World of Cinema Hami

pages.title:
AEROWATCH 1942 CALENDRIER COMPLET: DOUBLE MOON PHASE AND TIMELESS ELEG
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: AEROWATCH 1942 CALENDRIER COMPLET: DOUBLE MOON PHASE AND TIMELESS ELEG – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping For over a century, Aerowatch has been dedicated to creating watches that combine precision, design, and tradition. Its iconic 1942 collection now welcomes a new addition: the 1942 Calendrier Complet, a watch that stands out for its double moon phase complication, a true gem for mechanical watch enthusiasts. This model pays tribute to the moon-phase watches that marked the renaissance of mechanical timepieces in the 1980s. Double Moon Phase and Full Calendar The most striking feature of the 1942 Calendrier Complet is its double moon phase, designed to display the moon’s position simultaneously in both hemispheres of the Earth. Located at 6 o’clock against a starry background, this complication allows the wearer to track the moon phase for both the Northern and Southern Hemispheres, showcasing the Swiss manufacture’s precision and originality. In addition to the double moon phase, this watch offers a full calendar with day, date, and month indications. The months and days appear through central apertures, while the date is indicated by a grey-hand with a red half-moon tip, highlighting attention to detail in both functionality and aesthetics. The silver dial with a grained center and clear black Arabic numerals is complemented by elegant blue hour, minute, and second hands that glide smoothly across the dial. Automatic Movement and High Watchmaking The 1942 Calendrier Complet is powered by a Sellita SW 383-1 automatic movement , 11 ½’’’, with 25 jewels and 28,800 vibrations per hour, offering a 56-hour power reserve. This mechanism ensures precision and reliability and can be admired through the transparent sapphire case back, revealing the finely decorated rotor and artistically finished movement, reflecting the high standards of Swiss Haute Horlogerie. Timeless Design, Comfort, and Exclusivity The 42 mm stainless steel 316L case is slightly curved, providing perfect visual balance and a comfortable fit on the wrist. The domed sapphire crystal with anti-reflective coating protects the dial while enhancing its elegance, and the genuine black leather strap with quilted stitching and a folding clasp completes this classic, timeless design. The watch is also water-resistant up to 50 meters, ensuring durability and versatility for daily wear. In summary, the Aerowatch 1942 Calendrier Complet combines the tradition of Swiss watchmaking with modern precision, offering a refined and sophisticated design, perfect for those seeking a timepiece that is both functional and a true work of art. pages.title:
BORROWED TIME: THE VINTAGE WATCH DESIGN THAT QUIETLY RETURNED TO THE O
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: BORROWED TIME: THE VINTAGE WATCH DESIGN THAT QUIETLY RETURNED TO THE O – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The Oscar red carpet unfolds at a moment when nothing has been decided yet, but everything is already being judged. The focus settles almost entirely, and often superficially on the nominees. The winners haven’t been announced. The speeches haven’t been given. Their futures, at least in the official sense, remain unwritten. For now, all eyes are on them on how they’ve chosen, or allowed someone else to choose, to present themselves to the world. This is when the photographs matter most. Photographs preserve the details that will soon be dissected, circulated, and absorbed into the endless scroll. A cuff pulled back slightly. A hand adjusting a jacket. And occasionally, on the wrist, something that doesn’t quite belong to the present. Not the newest watch. Something older. Or something that looks older. Something that has already lived through another era entirely. Timothée Chalamet and the Shape of Endurance When Timothée Chalamet arrived at the 2025 Academy Awards wearing a custom butter-yellow Givenchy suit, his watch had already existed for three decades. It was a Cartier Baignoire, reference 1960, produced in 1994 as part of Cartier’s Byzantine Collection. The design resists easy placement. Oval, asymmetrical, and slightly disorienting, it came from a period when Cartier was experimenting with its own archive reinterpreting earlier forms rather than abandoning them. By the time Chalamet wore it, the watch had outlasted entire fashion cycles. It had existed long enough to lose any connection to trend. What remained was form. And memory. Rami Malek and the Return of the Pasha Rami Malek attended the 2020 Academy Awards wearing a vintage gold Pasha de Cartier from 1993, paired with a black Saint Laurent tuxedo. The leather strap and warm gold case gave the watch a quiet presence beneath the cuff. The Pasha’s origins reach back further still, to a design commissioned in the 1940s. Its defining feature the protective cap chained to the crown was created to seal the watch against water and dust. The structure remained intact decades later. It still worked exactly as it was meant to. Malek’s appearance also preceded Cartier’s relaunch of the Pasha collection later that year, reconnecting the watch with the contemporary moment without altering its identity. Michelle Yeoh and the Compression of Time Michelle Yeoh’s long association with Richard Mille has unfolded alongside a career spanning more than forty years. Richard Mille produces watches in extremely limited numbers, with production runs that end quickly and rarely return. Their scarcity becomes part of their timeline. When Yeoh wore the Richard Mille RM 07-02 at the 2023 Academy Awards, paired with a custom Dior Couture gown, the watch had already entered that narrower existence. The case, machined from sapphire crystal over the course of forty days, was outlined with diamond-set indices anchored by hand-finished gold prongs. Beneath the surface, the movement continued forward without interruption. Yeoh has continued to wear Richard Mille watches, carrying their timeline alongside her own. Elle Fanning and the Return of Ornament Elle Fanning’s Cartier Zelda HPI01765, worn at the 2025 Oscars, belonged to a category modern watchmak

pages.title:
CELEBRATING WOMEN THROUGH TIME: INTERNATIONAL WOMEN’S DAY WITH RADO
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: CELEBRATING WOMEN THROUGH TIME: INTERNATIONAL WOMEN’S DAY WITH RADO – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping On International Women’s Day, we celebrate the women who shape our world through talent, ambition, and determination. From sport to culture and entrepreneurship, women continue to inspire new generations and redefine what excellence looks like. For decades, Rado has been synonymous with innovation, elegance, and forward-thinking design. The brand’s ambassadors reflect those same values—women whose stories embody resilience, creativity, and confidence. Below, we highlight three inspiring personalities who represent Rado around the world. Natural Balance — Ashleigh Barty Few athletes have left such a lasting impression on tennis as Ashleigh Barty . The Australian champion reached World No.1 at just 23 years old , becoming one of the most respected players of her generation. Barty joined the Rado family in 2017 through the brand’s YoungStar program , and her relationship with the Swiss watchmaker has grown ever since. Her career highlights include Grand Slam victories such as Wimbledon and the Australian Open , before announcing her retirement in 2022 while still at the top of the sport. Beyond competition, Barty has collaborated with Rado on several special timepieces. Her philosophy — often summarized as “you are either winning or learning” — reflects the resilience and balance that define both elite athletes and exceptional watchmaking. Learn more about Ashleigh Barty → Inner Determination — Alysha Newman Canadian Olympian Alysha Newman represents a new generation of athletes who combine performance with creativity and entrepreneurship. Known globally for her achievements in pole vaulting , she is also an entrepreneur and advocate for athlete empowerment. Newman joined Rado as a Friend of the Brand , reflecting shared values of innovation, precision, and resilience. She is also Chief Talent Officer and founding partner of Hyper Talent , an AI-powered platform helping athletes and creators build sustainable careers. Through sport, business, and mentorship, Newman demonstrates how modern athletes are shaping the future far beyond the track. Elegance in Every Moment — Katrina Kaif Global icon Katrina Kaif brings elegance and international appeal to the Rado family. Known for her influential presence in film and fashion, she represents the sophisticated side of modern watch design. As a Rado ambassador, she frequently showcases pieces such as the Centrix Diamonds , a collection that combines refined aesthetics with the brand’s pioneering use of high-tech ceramic materials. The partnership highlights Rado’s ability to blend timeless beauty with contemporary design , celebrating watches as expressions of identity and personal style. Learn more about Katrina Kaif → Celebrating Women, Celebrating Time International Women’s Day reminds us that progress is driven by individuals who dare to lead, innovate, and inspire. Through ambassadors like Ashleigh Barty, Alysha Newman, and Katrina Kaif , Rado celebrates women who embody strength, elegance, and purpose. Because time is not only something we measure, it is something we shape. We aim to build a vibrant community where people can discover new brands and meet jewellers and aficionados passionate about timepieces and their stories. Here at WatchDNA, we recognize how essential it is to have all the knowledge required before purchasing. While our goal is to promote the item sold by our jewellery and brand partners, we strongly encourage everyone to learn as much as possible about each brand. Our brand portfolio constantly evolves, and we always search for new names to add. All items on our website are offered either directly by one of our jewelry partners or by the brand itself. You can follow the provided link to order directly from their website, or if you prefer, feel free to contact your nearest store to try them out in person. For added convenience, we also offer the service of transferring pre-selected items to the WatchDNA jeweller closest to you. While we strive to collaborate with local businesses, we also maintain strong relationships with certain manufacturers. Please be advised that for specific brands, we will redirect you directly to their own e-commerce platforms, and as a result, those brands cannot be physically tried in person. In addition, we take pride in making a positive impact on society, which is why we have partnered with exceptional nonprofits, and continually looking for additional local organizations to support. pages.content: Casio Computer Corp. – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Casio Computer Corp. CASIO COMPUTER CO. History ARTICLES Casio Computer Co. was founded in 1946 by Tadao Kashio as a calculator manufacturing company and entered the timepiece market in 1974 with the release of the CASIOTRON, their first digital quartz watch. This innovative device could display hours, minutes, seconds, and the date, marking Casio’s emergence as a significant player in the digital watch industry, a place it continues to uphold today. In 1983, Casio revolutionized the watch market with the introduction of the best-selling G-Shock series and continued to innovate in the following decades with watch lines including the Pro Trek series for outdoor enthusiasts, featuring altimeters, barometers, and compasses. The company also developed the Edifice line of analog watches, combining sophisticated styling with advanced technology. Today, Casio remains one of the world’s biggest manufacturers of digital watches, with the G-Shock line alone having sold over 100 million units globally. It continues to innovate with new features like Bluetooth connectivity and solar power while revisiting classic designs for re-issues and anniversary editions. Brands summary: Casio’s greatest creation, The G-Shock line, launched in 1983, revolutionized digital watches with its unique shock-resistant design and rugged aesthetic, establishing a new category in the watch market. It continues to enjoy massive popularity worldwide and now includes dozens of models from simple retro styles to luxurious all-metal versions. Casio 's main watch line, meanwhile, offers affordable, reliable timepieces ranging from simple digital watches like the iconic A158W to more feature-rich models at accessible price points. Oceanus , Casio’s luxury division, is distinguished by watches that combine high-end materials with advanced technology. The brand is also well-known for its innovative smartwatches, which offer fitness tracking and other features. pages.content: CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD History ARTICLES Citizen Watch Co. Ltd. was established by Kamekichi Yamazaki in 1918 as the Shokosha Watch Research Institute and produced its first timepiece — a pocket watch — in 1924. The name Citizen was chosen in 1930 to reflect the brand’s goal of making reliable watches at affordable prices, a legacy it continues to uphold a century later. Citizen produced Japan's first shock-resistant watch, the Parashock, in 1956, and Japan's first water-resistant watch, the Parawater, in 1959. The company’s biggest breakthrough came in 1976 with the introduction of Eco-Drive, a technology that converts both natural and artificial light into energy to power watches, eliminating the need for battery replacement. Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Citizen continued to innovate with the development of radio-controlled watches and titanium cases while refining their Eco-Drive technology. The company expanded globally through strategic acquisitions, purchasing American watch company Bulova in 2008, Swiss movement manufacturer La Joux-Perret in 2012, and Swiss watch manufacturer Frederique Constant in 2016. Today, Citizen remains one of the world's largest watch manufacturers, and is respected equally for mechanical watches like the Tsuyosa and a wide range of Eco-Drive models. Brands summary: Citizen is known for its affordable, innovative timepieces along with their advances in Eco-Drive technology, Super Titanium, and satellite timekeeping. Its portfolio of brands, including Frederique Constant , Bulova , Alpina , Arnold & Son , Campanola and Accutron represents a wide variety of forms, functions, and historical legacies. At the high end is Frederique Constant , which was founded in 1988, and is known for offering Swiss timepieces with in-house movements — including, in recent years, a tourbillon — at relatively accessible prices. Alpina likewise brings Swiss-made credentials to the table with a line of professional outdoor timepieces characterized by their "4" antimagnetic, anti-shock, water-resistant, and stainless steel standards. The addition of American watchmaker Bulova , best known for its ties to NASA and its high-frequency movements, as well as its former sub-brand Accutron , which was relaunched independently in 2020, add depth and variety to the Citizen Group’s. With the acquisition of Manufacture La Joux-Perret, a respected Swiss movement-maker, Citizen Group also gained control of Arnold & Son , an historic English brand specializing in high-end complications. Established in 1764, it carries forward the legacy of John Arnold, a visionary watchmaker renowned for his groundbreaking work in marine chronometers. Accutron
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Accutron – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Accutron ACCUTRON WATCH Official History ARTICLES Accutron, a pioneering watch brand, is celebrated for its innovation and precision in the world of timekeeping. Introduced by Bulova in 1960, Accutron revolutionized watchmaking with the introduction of the first fully electronic watch. Its groundbreaking technology, the tuning fork movement, allowed for unprecedented accuracy by using a resonating tuning fork instead of the traditional mechanical movement found in watches of the era. This innovation not only enhanced timekeeping accuracy but also influenced the development of electronic watches moving forward. The Accutron brand quickly became synonymous with precision, innovation, and technology, capturing the attention of watch enthusiasts and professionals alike. It was so reliable that it was used in the early days of America's space program, further cementing its place in history as a symbol of American innovation and technological advancement. Today, Accutron continues to be celebrated for its historical significance and continues to innovate with new designs and technologies, blending modern advancements with its rich heritage to create timepieces that are both timeless and forward-thinking. Alpina
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Alpina – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Alpina ALPINA WATCHES Official History ARTICLES Alpina is a premium watchmaking company that has been operating out of Geneva, Switzerland for more than 135 years. Recognizable by its signature red triangle, Alpina watches are made in Switzerland. Patents and innovative calibres are only two of the many firsts that Alpina, a true pioneer in the Swiss watchmaking industry, is responsible for. Alpina is a genuine trailblazer in the industry. Alpina is widely regarded as the company that first introduced the notion of a Swiss sports watch when it released its now-iconic Alpina 4 model in the year 1938. 1883 Gottlieb Hauser founds the "Alpina Union Horlogère S.A (UH) / Schweizerische Uhrmacher-Genossenschaft (SUG)" ("Corporation of Swiss Watchmakers"). His vision was the "Alpinist Principle": uniting Swiss watchmaking industry forces to accomplish the highest manufacturing quality and technological innovation under the most favourable conditions. The Union soon began manufacturing its own calibres. 1890 The Union relocates its headquarters to Biel/Bienne, where prominent member Jacob Straub operated his calibre factory (J. Straub & Co). At that time, the Geneva-based Alpina Ebauches factory, owned by Duret & Colonnaz, manufactured ébauches. 1900 The Alpina Union Horlogère takes part in the 1900 Universal Exposition in Paris and exhibits its calibres and creations to a distinguished and international audience. 1901 Jacob Straub registers the trademark "Alpina" to safeguard his previously unmarked 12- and 19-line calibres. As J. Straub & Co. is the Union's primary calibre supplier, virtually all high-end timepieces now bear the renowned "Alpina" insignia on their movements. As a result, Alpina was initially used as a protection and quality mark for exceptional movements. 1903 Gottlieb Hauser and his son undertake a perilous mission to Russia in order to establish a stronghold in the Tsarist empire. At the conclusion of their 50-day journey, their ledgers are filled with the names of a surprising number of new customers, some of whom are renowned. In 1955, Gottlieb Hauser Jr. penned "From Bienne to Astrakhan: The Adventures of a Swiss Watch Representative in Russia Fifty Years Ago" as an account of this journey. 1908 In celebration of its 25th anniversary, the Union registers the "Alpina" watch brand. For reasons of prestige, the Union members, the "Alpinists", agreed to use the brand "Alpina" only for watches that housed specified high-quality calibres, such as those with Breguet hairsprings and compensating balances. As was customary during this era, a potent name required a unique insignia; thus, the legendary Alpina red triangle emblem, the "Alpiner," was created. 1909 The Alpina Union Horlogère establishes the "Pracisions-Uhrenfabrik Alpina Glashütte i/S" in Glashütte, Germany, to manufacture watches. Geneva, Biel, Besançon (F), and Glashütte were now home to the Union's factories. 1910 The Alpina Union Horlogère produces a perpetual calendar complications for pocket watches. A significant technical obstacle for the time period. 1910 In its Glashütte facility, Alpina produces its first chronograph. In Switzerland, these timepieces were finely adjusted by W.A. Dubois, a renowned expert in his field. On their dials and mechanisms, these costly item

The company is known for its precision timepieces, including both mechanical and quartz watches, and for its innovations in watchmaking technology. Bulova has a long history of producing watches for the military and for the aviation industry, and it is also a popular brand among consumers for its classic and stylish designs.
